name: Coconut Flan with Caramel (Banh Dua Ca Ra Men)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fresh or canned coconut milk
recipeInstructions:
This is the ultimate coconut dessert—an adaptation of the classic "Creme renversee", or "Flan au caramel". The technique used is distinctly French, but the flavors are all Vietnamese. This custard is at its best when prepared a day in advance and refrigerated so the flavors can mellow. If you just can't wait, try the Vietnamese method of rapid cooling: place a small scoop of shaved ice on top of each custard before serving!
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Make the caramel:
- Cook sugar in a small heavy saucepan over low heat, swirling pan constantly, until brown.
- Stir hot water into the caramel, being careful to guard against splattering (mixture will bubble vigorously).
- Boil mixture, swirling pan occasionally, until sugar is thoroughly dissolved, about 2 minutes.
3. Pour caramel syrup into a 1-quart souffle dish or five 4-ounce ramekins. Tilt molds to coat all surfaces with caramel.
4. Make the custard:
- Combine coconut milk, milk, and sugar in a medium saucepan over low heat. Scald until sugar dissolves completely. Remove from heat.
- Whisk eggs and vanilla in large bowl. Gradually whisk hot coconut milk mixture into eggs, blending thoroughly.
- Strain custard through fine sieve into bowl. Carefully pour into caramel-lined souffle dish or ramekins.
5. Line a large roasting pan with 2 layers of paper towels. Place souffle dish in pan and add hot water to reach halfway up the side of dish. Bake in oven's center for 50 minutes (30 minutes if using ramekins) until knife inserted into center comes out clean. Do not disturb custard while baking.
6. Remove souffle dish from hot water. Allow to cool in cold-water bath.
7. Chill thoroughly. Run a knife around edge of custard and turn onto dessert plates. Serve with shaved ice or whipped cream, if desired.
Note: Paper towels in roasting pan allow hot water to circulate under souffle dish while baking for even heat distribution. Also, they stabilize small ramekins and keep them from moving around while baking.
Yield: 5 servings.
